text: The patient is positioned in a modified lithotomy position under spinal anesthesia. The surgeon assesses the size and extent of the anterior rectocele before repair, and injects adrenalized saline to minimize bleeding and facilitate dissection.

The surgeon makes an incision in the vaginal mucosa, starting proximally at the apex of the rectocele and extending distally to the posterior fornix. Dissection is performed in the proper plane to separate the mucosa from the underlying rectovaginal septum.
